name: Lafayette Tower CAT Entertainment Host

securitySheaf.label: Credential Probe
securitySheaf.effect: Badge, route, and activity-profile verification begins.

securitySheaf.label: Trace Lattice
securitySheaf.effect: CAT correlation systems start linking deck signature, route, and possible physical location.

securitySheaf.label: Scramble / Rights Collapse
securitySheaf.effect: Directories, schedules, and media assets remix; decoy routes multiply.

securitySheaf.label: Handler Tar Baby
securitySheaf.effect: Producer and handler icons try to trap the decker in approval loops and focus tests.

securitySheaf.label: Killer / Blaster Response
securitySheaf.effect: Hard security icons engage with dangerous force.

securitySheaf.label: Building Security Crosslink
securitySheaf.effect: Physical Lafayette Tower security receives correlated alerts.

encounter.consequence: Building Security Crosslink: physical Lafayette Tower security may receive camera, elevator, door, or lobby-staff alerts. Notify the GM if this remains unresolved.

securitySheaf.label: Ultraviolet Containment Protocol
securitySheaf.effect: The host seals routes and brings UV-native constructs or psychotropic pressure to bear.

securitySheaf.label: CAT Executive Security Event
securitySheaf.effect: CAT treats the intrusion as a strategic compromise, not a local alert.

sculpting: A vertical entertainment-corporate arcology: glass atriums, marquees, executive elevators, gold-record rooms, focus-test theaters, prototype simsense rigs, rigging catwalks, and a hidden black-stage Ultraviolet basement.
notes: Local Cross Applied Technology entertainment-division host at Lafayette Tower. Extremely dangerous. Public side is visitor-safe corporate theater; secure side exposes subsystems and hidden R&D. The Ultraviolet layer is hidden inside the same profile and should reveal only after deep-host success or story permission.

nodes.title: Music Circle Grid Approach
nodes.description: Lafayette Tower rises from the Music Circle grid like a sealed vertical studio campus. The public face is beautiful; the private access surface is corporate-grade and hostile.

nodes.title: Public Glass Atrium
nodes.description: The public host surface is all glass, music, visitor kiosks, corporate sizzle reels, sanitized division language, and polished hospitality avatars. It is impressive, but it is not where the secrets live.

choices.label: Check visitor and vendor intake kiosks
Return to the Music Circle grid

nodes.title: Public Demo Reels
nodes.description: Official CAT demo reels show immersive concerts, brand-safe simsense previews, and audience engagement metrics stripped of anything actionable. Tell the GM only if you need public-facing cover language.

nodes.title: Visitor and Vendor Kiosks
nodes.description: Vendor intake forms, visitor badges, and polished contact surfaces route legitimate traffic toward human approval. Nothing here gives private access by itself.

nodes.title: Sanitized Talent Pitch Theater
nodes.description: A public pitch theater explains how CAT helps artists become brands, brands become experiences, and experiences become lifestyle ecosystems. It is blandly sinister but officially harmless.

nodes.title: Secure Credential Gate
nodes.description: A brushed-steel credential gate checks media, legal, production, security, and executive authorization chains. Failure here should reveal almost nothing about the private topology.

choices.label: Back out to the Music Circle grid

nodes.title: Private Entertainment-Division Hub
nodes.description: You are inside the private stack. Executive elevators, contract vaults, analytics theaters, production controls, and R&D routing all branch from here under tight CAT scrutiny.

choices.label: Back out through the secure credential gate

nodes.title: Licensing and Contract Records
nodes.description: You appear to have access to artist, vendor, licensing, royalty, and brand-partner records. Tell the GM you have contract/licensing access; ask what names, dates, partners, or rights categories can be searched.

choices.label: Search legal routing for R&D project names
Attempt a contract or release-control alteration

nodes.title: Talent Analytics and Behavioral Models
nodes.description: You appear to have access to audience segmentation, talent viability models, image-manipulation profiles, and behavioral response testing. Tell the GM you have analytics access; ask what segments, artists, campaigns, or test windows can be searched.

choices.label: Trace prototype links behind the focus-test mirrors
Search for a hidden basement metaphor

nodes.title: Production and Building-Slave Systems
nodes.description: You appear to have access to studio routing, rehearsal-space controls, cameras, elevators, doors, badge readers, environmental systems, and command catwalks. Tell the GM you have production/building control access; confirm exact physical scope before assuming control.

choices.label: Use a service-control route toward R&D

nodes.title: Restricted Prototype R&D Routing
nodes.description: You appear to have access to restricted R&D routing: unfinished sets, simsense prototypes, sensory rigs, project names, and internal approvals. Tell the GM you have prototype-routing access; ask what project categories and codenames are visible.

choices.label: Extract project names from routing metadata
Locate the hidden basement UV seam
Force progress through serious corporate IC

nodes.title: Project Routing Constellation
nodes.description: Project names, legal gates, routing exceptions, creative departments, and security notes glimmer in a constellation that refuses to admit it has a lowest floor. Make a note: CAT project routing is visible here; ask the GM which codenames can be read.

nodes.title: Rights Collapse Console
nodes.description: A protected console can alter contracts, release controls, talent models, or research routing. Any edit threatens to collapse permissions into alert state. Permanent changes must be reported to the GM.

choices.label: Commit a high-value alteration and package proof
Back out before handler loops pin you

nodes.title: Building System Scope Note
nodes.description: You identify likely camera, elevator, badge, or studio-control linkages. Tell the GM you found building-system access pointers; confirm which physical systems Lafayette Tower actually exposes through this host.

choices.label: Log off with the building-scope note

nodes.title: Paydata: Contract Leverage
nodes.description: You recover contract, royalty, or brand-partner leverage. Tell the GM you recovered Lafayette contract paydata; exact targets, value, and consequences need GM handling.

choices.label: Log off with the contract paydata

nodes.title: Paydata: Influence Models
nodes.description: You recover audience segmentation, talent-shaping, or behavioral-response model data. Tell the GM you recovered influence-model paydata and ask how explosive it is.

choices.label: Log off with the influence-model paydata

nodes.title: Paydata: Production-System Linkage
nodes.description: You recover proof of studio, demo, camera, elevator, or building-control linkages. Tell the GM you recovered production-system linkage paydata for valuation and operational follow-up.

nodes.title: Paydata: Prototype Entertainment R&D
nodes.description: You recover prototype entertainment, simsense, and immersive-media R&D records. Tell the GM you recovered prototype R&D paydata; exact project names and value are GM-facing.

choices.label: Log off with prototype R&D paydata

nodes.title: Permanent Outcome: Rights / Release Alteration
nodes.description: You alter a protected rights, release, routing, or talent-model record. Notify the GM immediately: this is a permanent campaign-state change. Tell the GM what category was changed and what cover signature or false approval was used.

choices.label: Log off before the rights collapse tightens

nodes.title: Exfiltration Edit Bay
nodes.description: You have material the crew can use: contracts, project names, behavioral models, prototype notes, building hooks, or proof that the tower is deeper than it admits. Tell the GM which categories you actually recovered.

choices.label: Log off through a clean edit bay render

nodes.title: Black Stage Door Below the Floor Count
nodes.description: A door appears where the topology says no door can be: black stage paint, cold brass handle, no label, and an elevator tone from somewhere beneath the basement. This is not ordinary host architecture.

choices.label: Mark the seam and retreat before containment notices

nodes.title: Ultraviolet White Room
nodes.description: The Matrix stops acting like menus. A blank test room breathes, then becomes a stage, an audience, a pitch meeting, and a memory of a song you have not heard yet. Tell the GM you reached the Lafayette UV layer.

choices.label: Survive the host-native creative director construct
Read the room as an entertainment prototype instead of a file tree
Retreat through the black stage door

nodes.title: Living Stage Set
nodes.description: Sets rewrite themselves in response to attention. Prototype personalities, impossible audiences, and recursive media scenes test how influence becomes entertainment.

choices.label: Extract UV project codenames and influence-tech evidence
Hijack a symbolic production control in the UV layer
Escape before the host learns too much about you

nodes.title: UV Project Core
nodes.description: This is campaign-grade paydata: hidden project names, prototype media logic, executive culpability, and evidence of CAT capabilities beneath Lafayette Tower. Tell the GM you cracked the UV Project Core; the GM will provide the actual details.

choices.label: Exfiltrate the UV proof through hostile containment
Stay for one more impossible answer

nodes.title: Hard Cut to Black
nodes.description: The tower's spotlights wink out one by one. If the trace lattice did not firm up, CAT may only know that something briefly interrupted the show.
